category_title: Reserved and anxious students with writing avoidance
minimal_context: The student is expected to participate in a whole-class read-aloud discussion about mythical characters in Eric Carle’s poetry collection, Dragons, Dragons.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are going to have a whole-class read-aloud discussion about the mythical characters in Eric Carle’s poetry collection, Dragons, Dragons.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits in the back of the group, fiddling with books on the shelf, and rarely looking up.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Lydia, what do you think about the mythical characters we just read about?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Faces away during the discussion and begins squirming.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please share your thoughts with the group.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Keeps her head down on her desk, staring into space, and does not speak a word.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are going to have a whole-class read-aloud discussion about the mythical characters in Eric Carle’s poetry collection, Dragons, Dragons.

STUDENT: (Sits in the back of the group, fiddling with books on the shelf, and rarely looking up.)

TUTOR: Lydia, what do you think about the mythical characters we just read about?

STUDENT: (Faces away during the discussion and begins squirming.)

TUTOR: Please share your thoughts with the group.

STUDENT: (Keeps her head down on her desk, staring into space, and does not speak a word.)

task.content: Participating in a read-aloud discussion of mythical characters in Eric Carle’s poetry collection, Dragons, Dragons

context.environment: Whole-class read aloud on the rug or at her desk
context.materials: Eric Carle’s poetry collection, Dragons, Dragons; books on the shelf; writing notebook
context.time_context: During the first 6 weeks of school until late November

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Stayed in her seat or she sat in the back of the group, fiddling with books on the shelf, and rarely looking at Mae; did not hear her speak a word

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Facing away from Mae during the entire discussion, head down on her desk, squirming or staring into space
